doc_content.summary: stark-review is a complex orchestration skill that performs parallel, multi-agent code reviews on Pull Requests. It dispatches 18 specialized agents (3 LLMs across 6 domains), aggregates and classifies their findings to filter out noise, and runs an autonomous loop to fix code, run regression tests, and push commits. It finalizes by posting a high-signal summary to the PR and auto-creating GitHub tickets for unfixed severe issues.
key_features: Parallel multi-agent dispatch (Claude, Codex, Gemini via 6 domain prompts).
Autonomous fix-test-push loop (up to a configurable number of rounds).
Sophisticated noise filtering and finding classification (Fix vs False Positive).
Detailed observability with 5-minute checkpoints and per-phase metrics.
Automatic generation of GitHub Bug Issues for unresolved high-severity findings.

alt_text: Architecture and workflow diagram for the stark-review skill showing the multi-agent code review process, including setup, the autonomous review-fix loop, summary generation, and GitHub posting.
